title: Humcha Jain temples
tagline: Humcha Jain temples — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Humcha Jain temples or Humcha basadis are a group of temples found in Humcha village of Shimoga district in Karnataka, India.
overview.description: The Humcha Jain temples or Humcha basadis are a group of temples found in Humcha village of Shimoga district in Karnataka, India. They were constructed in the 7th century CE in the period of the Santara dynasty and are regarded as one of the major Jain centres of Karnataka. The Padmavati Basadi is the most well-known of these temples.

topPlaces.name: Humcha
topPlaces.description: Humcha / Hombuja is a small town near Ripponpet, Hosanagara taluk in Shimoga district in the Indian state of Karnataka. Humbaja Atishaya Jain Teerth Kshetra is famous for being home to an ancient temple of Goddess Padmavati, and to the Humbaj Matha (seminary/monastery), an important institution of the Jain community.

topPlaces.name: Adavalli
topPlaces.description: Adavalli is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Hosanagara taluk of Shimoga district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Ambuthirtha
topPlaces.description: Ambuthirtha is a culturally significant mountain located approximately 15 km (9 miles) from the village of Thirthahalli, in the Shimoga District of Karnataka, India. It is the source of the Sharavathi river.

topPlaces.name: Jambe, Mulshi
topPlaces.description: Jambe is a village in Mulshi taluka of Pune District in the state of Maharashtra, India.

items.note: Humcha / Hombuja is a small town near Ripponpet, Hosanagara taluk in Shimoga district in the Indian state of Karnataka. Humbaja Atishaya Jain Teerth Kshetra is famous for being home to an ancient temple of Goddess Padmavati, and to the Humbaj Matha (seminary/monastery), an important institution of the Jain community.

Ambuthirtha is a culturally significant mountain located approximately 15 km (9 miles) from the village of Thirthahalli, in the Shimoga District of Karnataka, India. It is the source of the Sharavathi river.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Humcha Jain temples?
faq.a: The best time to visit Humcha Jain temples is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Humcha Jain temples?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (106 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (118 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Humcha Jain temples?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Humcha Jain temples at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Humcha Jain temples?
faq.a: Stays in Humcha Jain temples start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Humcha Jain temples?
faq.a: The top attractions are Humcha, Adavalli, Ambuthirtha, Jambe, Mulshi.
